name: sunflower stuffed poblanos
instructions:
Mix cream cheese, sunflower seeds, garlic powder, onion powder, new mexican chili powder, paprika, and black pepper in a bowl until smooth. Halve and seed the poblanos, then stuff with the cream cheese mixture. Roast in a 400°F oven until the poblanos are tender and the filling is golden on top.

OPTIONAL (HIGHER EFFORT): pre-roast the poblanos first, peel off the charred skin, then stuff and finish-roast. Smoother texture, deeper poblano flavor, but more work.
notes:
A lower-effort sibling to `massaman-stuffed-poblanos`. Both recipes capitalize on the same underlying affinity: cream cheese pairs well with poblano peppers (the dairy-forward fat melds with the pepper's smoky/vegetal character) AND with sunflower seeds (cream cheese + sunflower is the canonical bagel-and-toast combination — see the nuts-and-seeds article). This recipe leans into both at once.

VS MASSAMAN STUFFED POBLANOS: the massaman version uses white beans + massaman curry paste for a more substantial, indian-leaning stuffed pepper. This one drops the beans and curry paste entirely and goes pure dairy-and-seed — faster prep, lighter result, more american register.

SERVING — PAIRS WITH WESTERN SALMON DISHES: the cream cheese filling gives the dish a natural pairing with western salmon preparations (french leek-and-cream salmon, lemon-butter salmon, gravlax-adjacent). The cream cheese on the salmon plate and the cream cheese inside the pepper anchor the meal around the same fat profile without competing.
